posture: Quiet midweek scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm, and 1 July brought the staged NSW and Victorian FOGO/kerbside-standardisation milestones, but these are regulatory-rollout dates rather than fresh innovation signals; the week's strongest 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a–§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Several recurring or undated items were considered and excluded: the staged NSW FOGO mandate and the Victorian household waste/recycling standardisation (e.g. South Gippsland introducing FOGO kerbside) both commence 1 July 2026, but these are regulatory-rollout milestones rather than fresh innovation signals and trace to earlier EPA/state-government coverage, so were not logged; a Queensland waste-and-recycling workforce/economic study surfaced around 1 July but is sector statistics, not an innovation signal; ongoing NSW AI-in-planning activity (the multi-council AI trial, DAISY at Wingecarribee) produced no new dated development today; recycled-content road/pavement material (City of Sydney geopolymer at Alexandria, Reconophalt/Hume, Sutherland Shire) surfaced only via older or undated coverage; and a widely-shared 'flying bin drone' waste-collection trial in Milton Keynes (UK) was traced to a 1 April 2025 satirical article and excluded. Today is a Thursday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
